notes: Resolve-never-materialize: ResolveWorkflowLayers applies DefaultWorkflow() at read time (workflow_resolve.go:12-19); tp init writes {} (init.go:68). No defaults frozen into task files.
Round-trip fidelity: WriteTaskFile marshals TaskFile.Workflow (WorkflowOverride, omitempty pointers) so absent fields stay absent; io_test.go:15-118 asserts round-trip equality and {}-normalization.

No stripping by value: write path serializes only present (non-nil) pointers via omitempty; clampWorkflowRanges drops out-of-range values only at resolution, never rewrites the file (engine/projectconfig.go:17-31). Present fields preserved.

Minimal surface, compile-enforced reroute: TaskFile.Workflow is WorkflowOverride (distinct from Workflow); effective reads route through EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (done.go:136,341,575; gate.go). No new command/flag.
No --unset: runSetWorkflow only accepts field=value pairs; no unset flag or removal path exists in set.go.
Precedence unchanged (task-override > project > built-in); config schema unchanged — knownWorkflowKeys (engine/projectconfig.go:63-67) is the v0.24.0 set incl. commit_strategy.
No new command/flag: init and set expose only pre-existing flags; the change is confined to serialization and init's sparse-block behavior.
No automatic thinning: StripTaskWorkflowFields runs only via user-invoked tp config --extract; no load/write path thins files automatically.
Built-in defaults unchanged and single-sourced: DefaultWorkflow() is the sole definition (2 clean rounds, 600s, no caps, no checks).

commit_strategy retained: --commit-strategy flag still writes tf.Workflow.CommitStrategy; field is presence-tracked (model/projectconfig.go:39) and round-tripped/resolved (configresolve.go:176). Not removed.

Settable set unchanged: editableWorkflowFields = five integers + checks; readOnlyWorkflowFields = quality_gate, commit_strategy (rejected at set.go:292-296). quality_gate/commit_strategy stay init-authored.

WorkflowOverride.CommitStrategy *string (model/projectconfig.go:39); parseWorkflowOverride reads commit_strategy (engine/projectconfig.go:99-105); ResolveWorkflowLayers uses pickString (configresolve.go:176). Test covers parse + override-wins/project-applies/default-empty resolution.

TaskFile.Workflow stored as WorkflowOverride (taskfile.go:11); ReadTaskFile applies no defaults (io.go:11-73); effective reads via resolver. Tests: RoundTrip preserves override incl quality_gate/commit_strategy/empty-checks, and no-workflow-key normalizes to {}. Command matrix binds through shared WriteTaskFile.

ResolveEffectiveWorkflow/EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile clamp the task override (configresolve.go:142,156); tests cover no-project-config resolving to 2 with 'out of range' warning, and project-config-present resolving to 3 (clamp does not mask project layer).

tp init writes WorkflowOverride{} (init.go:68); flags set only their field (init.go:76-83). Table test covers all four cases: no flags -> {}, quality-gate only, commit-strategy only, both compose; asserts no materialized defaults.

runSetWorkflow sets only the named pointer (set.go:355-374), validates before write (set.go:283-337). Test covers successful single-field set (no siblings), out-of-range exit 1, non-parseable exit 1, non-settable quality_gate exit 2, unknown field exit 2 — file unmodified on each rejection.

resolveConfigWorkflow clamps the override before source attribution (config.go:121); resolvedConfig reports commit_strategy (config.go:57). Tests cover clamped out-of-range -> source 'project' value 3 (never override), and §10.7 commit_strategy -> source 'override'. Round 1 fixes verified.

spec/0.25.0.tasks.json workflow block thinned to "workflow": {} and committed (only spec/0.26.0.md is untracked). The pre/post tp config --resolved equivalence check is a manual migration step, not independently re-verifiable from artifacts, but the thinned committed outcome is confirmed.

resolveConfigWorkflow clamps a function-local override copy (L121); errors propagated via output.Error/os.Exit with MalformedConfigError hint (L107-114). New commit_strategy row (L57) is read-only map building. LoadTaskWorkflowOverride error at L104 is intentional best-effort for a read-only informational command, pre-existing, ResolveEffectiveWorkflow re-detects malformed config.
Test file: t.TempDir, os.WriteFile 0o600, dirs 0o755, filepath.Join, require.NoError throughout. Type assertions guarded by controlled setup; a panic is a test failure.

Both sites (L136 single, L341 batch) call engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile(taskFilePath) which returns a model.Workflow value; .QualityGate != "" is a safe string compare, no nil-deref. No new swallowed error introduced here.
Test file: gateFailureMessage signature updated to take resolved *model.Workflow directly; json.Marshal error checked with require.NoError. No safety concern.
Test file: t.TempDir, filepath.Join, os.WriteFile 0o600, require.NoError/assert; asserts empty persisted workflow block. No safety concern.

Change writes model.WorkflowOverride{} (empty) as data only; unmarshal errors handled with os.Exit(ExitFile) (L63-66); write path model.WriteTaskFile(targetPath,tf) at L177 uses 0o600 with error checked.

WorkflowOverride{} empty block; address-of-local idiom (qg:=...; &qg at L77-78, cs at L81-82) is correct Go, not in a loop so no capture aliasing. WriteTaskFile 0o600 with error checked (L85-88). Existing Stat/WriteFile create path unchanged (no flock, consistent with init/create convention).
Test file: table-driven, t.TempDir, filepath.Join, os.WriteFile 0o600, os.ReadFile+require.NoError, json.Unmarshal checked. No safety concern.

Round-1 clamp fix verified safe: override:=tf.Workflow is a shallow copy of WorkflowOverride; clampWorkflowRanges only sets copy field pointers to nil (*p=nil), never dereferences-and-writes the shared underlying value, so tf.Workflow is not corrupted (no aliasing). *p!=nil guard (projectconfig.go L20) prevents nil-deref. ResolveWorkflowLayers returns a value, wfPtr points to local wf. Guarded by !planMinimal.
Test file: t.TempDir, filepath.Join, os.WriteFile 0o600, require.NoError, json.Unmarshal checked. Asserts out-of-range review_clean_rounds clamps to default in tp plan. No safety concern.

resolvedConfig adds commit_strategy line (57) and resolveConfigWorkflow adds ClampWorkflowRanges call (119-121) with a clear code comment citing the spec. All functions documented, under 80 lines, no unwrapped error propagation, no bare []T reaching JSON. Clean.
New test file: two focused tests with doc comments citing spec sections, idiomatic require/assert + t.TempDir/t.Chdir. No maintainability issues.

Two lines swapped to engine.EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile(taskFilePath).QualityGate (compiler-enforced effective-read reroute, §4.2); helper is documented and best-effort. Minor re-read of task file vs already-loaded tfPre is the sanctioned pattern, not a defect. runDoneSingle/runDoneMulti exceed ~110 lines but are pre-existing and untouched in substance by this change.
Unit tests build model.Workflow literals and assert gateFailureMessage; clean, descriptive names, no convention issues.
New-bare-array subtest updated to assert an empty workflow block (v0.26.0 no-materialization) with an explanatory comment; shared readPersistedWorkflow helper is well factored. Clean.

Workflow field for a new bare-array import changed from engine.DefaultWorkflow() to model.WorkflowOverride{}, matching the presence-preserving storage type (§3.6). DefaultWorkflow still used in configresolve.go, so no dead code left. Correct type.

Sparse workflow block (model.WorkflowOverride{}) with local qg/cs copies for pointer assignment (avoids aliasing the package globals) - idiomatic. runEjectRoles keeps written := make([]string,0) so the JSON slice never serializes null. All functions documented and under budget.
New table-driven test with a doc comment citing §10.1; clean case names, proper require/assert usage.

Round-1 fix: copies tf.Workflow, clamps the copy (no mutation of loaded struct), resolves via ResolveWorkflowLayers. wfPtr is *model.Workflow with json workflow,omitempty (22) so nil is omitted, not null - no null-vs-[] concern (and pointers are outside that rule). runPlan is 144 lines (over the ~110 cobra tolerance) but pre-existing; this change added only ~2 net lines. Clean.
New test with a doc comment; asserts tp plan clamps an out-of-range authored value to the built-in default, matching config/done/review. Idiomatic and self-explanatory.

config --resolved now reports commit_strategy (L57) alongside all 8 workflow fields; resolveConfigWorkflow clamps out-of-range override (L121) so a clamped field attributes to project/default, never override. Output is a map (json-sorted keys, deterministic); checks resolves to [] not null. R1 commit_strategy gap fixed.

Tests lock the contract: out-of-range review_clean_rounds=0 override attributes to source=project value=3 (L31-32), and a task-file commit_strategy attributes to source=override value=squash (L48-49). Both assertions pass at runtime.

Gate reads effective workflow via EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile (L136,341,575), which clamps the override internally before resolving — no effective read off the storage struct. QualityGate!="" gating and gate JSON shape unchanged; consistent with plan/config clamp.
Gate failure output contract held: timeout serializes exit_code as null (pointer *int, L20-22) and reports seconds; non-zero exit names the gate cmd. No []-vs-null or shape regression.

Import presence contract covered: bare-array/--force re-import preserves target workflow (L48-51), wrapped doc with workflow key wins (L77), and a new bare-array import writes an empty {} block with no materialized convergence/timeout fields (L93-95). Matches §3.6/§10.11.

Import preserves the target's existing workflow block when the source carries no workflow key (raw-key check before struct defaulting, L125-127); no default materialization. TaskFile.Workflow is a non-omitempty struct value so the container always serializes as {} object, never null.

tp init writes a sparse WorkflowOverride{} (L68), setting only quality_gate/commit_strategy when their flags are passed (L76-83). No convergence/timeout/checks defaults materialized; persisted block is {} object (verified at runtime).

Table test asserts the persisted workflow block equals exactly the flagged fields — {} for no flags, single field for each flag, composed for both (L22-25,L39) — no materialized defaults. Contract for §10.1 locked.

R1 PARTIAL fixed: runPlan now clamps the override (ClampWorkflowRanges, L161) before ResolveWorkflowLayers (L162), so tp plan reports the clamped effective value — verified live: out-of-range review_clean_rounds=0 resolves to project value 3, identical to config/done. Clamp mutates a value copy of tf.Workflow, no aliasing. workflow omitted in --minimal (nil *Workflow). Warning-to-stderr is suppressed, consistent with done.go's EffectiveWorkflowForTaskFile.
Test locks the fix: an authored out-of-range review_clean_rounds=0 is reported as clamped value 2 in tp plan stdout (L27), matching config/done/review; exit 0. Passes at runtime.
